title: Working in Barcelona, feeling like a foreigner
body:
## I love that we're an international tech hub. English is the tool, agreed. But... **can someone explain to me why 90% of job offers ignore Catalan and Spanish?**

It seems like we're designing a closed ecosystem for "expats". If we don't require local languages at work, how do we want new people to truly integrate into the city's culture?

We're not only losing identity, we're creating barriers instead of bridges.

We want global technology, but with local roots. **Because code has no language, but teams do**?
